nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pershale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Perschall, a variant of Parschall (see Parshall ); alternatively, a variant of Perschel, from a pet form of Persch, a short form of Berthold or Peter in the border area with Czechia.

variants.name: Parshall
meanings: Americanized form of South German Parschall, a variant of Parschalk, a status name for a freeholder (a freeman with few service obligations), from Middle High German bar ‘naked’, ‘free from, apparent’ + schalk ‘servant, man’. See Pershall .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard De Pershale was born in 1215, in England, United Kingdom, his father, John Swynnerton, was 8 and his mother, Eleanor de Pershale, was 30. He had at least 1 son with (Mrs. ) De Pershale. He died in 1250, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard De Pershale est né en 1215, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Swynnerton, avait 8 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Pershale, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec (Mrs. ) De Pershale. Il est décédé en 1250, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard De Pershale 1215 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Swynnerton 8 und seine Mutter Eleanor de Pershale 30. Er hatte mit (Mrs. ) De Pershale mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1250 im Alter von 35 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Pershale è nato il nel 1215 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Swynnerton, aveva 8 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor de Pershale, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con (Mrs. ) De Pershale. Morì nel 1250, all'età di 35 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Pershale(이)가 1215년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Swynnerton(은)는 8세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Pershale(은)는 30세였다. 그는 (Mrs. ) De Pershale(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1250년 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard De Pershale родился в 1215 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Swynnerton было 8 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Pershale, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от (Mrs. ) De Pershale. Он умер в 1250 г. в возрасте 35 лет.
